title: Goldfish Growth
description: What size can a goldfish reach and what factors influence its growth?
narrative: The user is seeking factual information about the typical maximum length of goldfish, the rate at which they grow, and variables that affect growth such as tank/pond size, diet, genetics, and species (single‑tail vs. fancy). Relevant documents should describe expected adult sizes, growth limitations due to confined spaces, and any related health implications.

title: Wi-Fi vs Bluetooth
description: How do Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth differ in technology, performance, and typical use cases?
narrative: The user seeks a concise comparison of Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth, covering their architectural models, data rates, range, common applications, and situations where one is preferred over the other. Relevant documents should explain each technology, contrast their key characteristics, and discuss complementary or alternative uses.

title: US WWI Entry
description: What were the reasons the United States voluntarily entered World War I?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive explanation of the motivations behind the United States' decision to join World War I, including political, economic, diplomatic, and public‑opinion factors such as unrestricted submarine warfare, the Zimmermann Telegram, the sinking of the Lusitania, propaganda efforts, and the shift from isolationism. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss these causes and how they collectively led to the U.S. declaration of war in 1917.

title: Declaratory Judgment Definition
description: What is the legal definition of a declaratory judgment and when can it be obtained?
narrative: The user seeks an authoritative explanation of a declaratory judgment, including its statutory basis, the circumstances that make a controversy justiciable, the purpose of the judgment (i.e., a court's declaratory opinion without enforcement), and any procedural requirements under U.S. law. Relevant documents should clearly define the term, describe the conditions for jurisdiction, and outline the effect and limits of a declaratory judgment.

title: Right Pelvic Pain Causes
description: What are the possible medical causes of right-sided pelvic pain in adults?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of conditions that can cause pain localized to the right pelvis, covering gynecologic, gastrointestinal, urologic, musculoskeletal, and referred sources. Relevant material should list specific diagnoses (e.g., ovarian cyst, hemorrhagic corpus luteum, appendicitis, diverticulitis, Crohn's disease, colon cancer, chronic constipation, inguinal hernia, interstitial cystitis, kidney stones, urinary tract infection, lung infection referral) and provide typical symptoms, prevalence, or diagnostic clues. Documents that do not address right‑pelvic specific etiologies or only discuss generic abdominal pain are not considered relevant.

title: Social Determinants
description: What are the social determinants of health and how do they influence health outcomes?
narrative: The user wants a thorough overview of the social determinants of health, including a clear definition, the major categories (e.g., income, education, employment, social support, housing, environment), and an explanation of how these factors shape individual and population health and contribute to health inequities. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss these determinants, describe their mechanisms of impact, and may present frameworks or models that organize them. Materials that only mention health without linking it to broader socioeconomic or environmental conditions are not relevant.

title: Legionella Pneumonia Link
description: Does Legionella pneumophila cause pneumonia?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative medical information confirming whether the bacterium Legionella pneumophila is a causative agent of pneumonia, including definitions of Legionnaires' disease, prevalence statistics, and clinical presentation. Relevant documents should explicitly state that L. pneumophila is responsible for Legionnaires' disease, which is a severe form of pneumonia, and provide supporting details such as symptom lists or epidemiological data. Documents that discuss unrelated illnesses or only mention Legionella without linking it to pneumonia are not relevant.

title: Jamaica Weather
description: What are the typical weather conditions in Jamaica throughout the year?
narrative: The user wants a general overview of Jamaica's climate, including average high/low temperatures, rainfall, humidity, and seasonal variations, as well as guidance on the most pleasant times to visit. Relevant documents should describe month‑by‑month weather patterns, temperature ranges for coastal areas (and note mountain differences), rainfall averages, and any advice on best travel periods, rather than providing real‑time forecasts.

title: Cerebral Palsy Dysarthria Types
description: What are the specific types of dysarthria associated with cerebral palsy?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive list and description of the dysarthria sub‑types that arise in individuals with cerebral palsy, including characteristic speech patterns, underlying neurological involvement, and distinguishing features. Relevant material should explicitly connect each dysarthria type to cerebral palsy and provide details on symptoms, affected muscle groups, and the affected areas of the nervous system.

title: Robert Gray
description: Who was Robert Gray and what were his notable achievements?
narrative: The user wants a concise biography of Captain Robert Gray, the American sea‑captain who sailed in the late 18th century. Relevant information should include his birth and death details, his command of the Lady Washington, his circumnavigation of the globe, his 1792 discovery and naming of the Columbia River, and how this exploration supported the United States’ claim to the Oregon Territory. Documents that provide factual data on his life, voyages, ships, and historical significance are considered relevant.

title: Sous Vide Foods
description: Which types of food can be cooked using the sous‑vide method?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive list of food categories and specific examples that are suitable for sous‑vide preparation, such as fish, various meats, eggs, pork, and other proteins. Relevant documents should mention particular foods, describe how they are cooked sous‑vide (including any temperature or timing details), and explain why they work well with this technique. Items that only discuss equipment, general cooking theory, or unrelated recipes are not relevant.

title: Flea Life Cycle
description: What is the total duration of a flea's life cycle from egg to adult, including variations by stage and environmental conditions?
narrative: The user wants a concise yet comprehensive answer that outlines the length of each developmental stage (egg, larva, pupa, adult) and the overall time span from hatching to maturity, noting how factors such as humidity, temperature, and host availability can extend or shorten the cycle. Relevant documents must provide specific time ranges for each stage and the total cycle, and discuss circumstances that cause the cycle to be as short as a few weeks or as long as several months.

title: Contour Plowing Benefits
description: What can contour plowing reduce?
narrative: The user is looking for information on the negative effects or problems that are mitigated by practicing contour plowing. Relevant documents should describe specific reductions achieved by contour plowing, such as decreased soil erosion, reduced water runoff, lower sediment loss, and improved water infiltration. Sources that only define contour plowing without mentioning its mitigating effects are not relevant.

title: Salvation Army Founding
description: When was the Salvation Army founded?
narrative: The user is looking for the exact year (and any supporting details) when the Salvation Army was established; relevant information must include the founding year, the founder (William Booth), and, if available, the location, resolving any conflicting dates found in sources.

title: Active Margin Definition
description: What is an active continental margin and how does it differ from a passive margin?
narrative: The user seeks a clear definition of an active margin in the context of plate tectonics, including its key characteristics (e.g., subduction of oceanic plate beneath a continent), typical geological activity (earthquakes, volcanism), and examples that illustrate the concept. Relevant documents should explain the concept, contrast it with passive margins, and may provide regional instances such as the western coast of North America.

title: RN vs BSN
description: What are the differences between a Registered Nurse (RN) license and a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) degree?
narrative: The user wants a clear comparison of the RN professional license and the BSN academic degree, including how they relate to each other, differences in education requirements, career pathways, salary potential, and management opportunities. Relevant documents should explain the meaning of RN, BSN, and ADN, clarify that RN is a license while BSN is an educational credential, and outline the practical implications of each.

title: Medicare Ventilation Definition
description: What is the official Medicare definition of mechanical ventilation?
narrative: The user seeks the precise definition of mechanical ventilation as used by Medicare for coverage and reimbursement purposes, including any associated coding or billing criteria. Relevant documents should contain Medicare policy language, statutory or regulatory definitions, and explanations of how mechanical ventilation is classified for Medicare claims.

title: Trapezoid Midsegment
description: How can I find the length of the midsegment of a trapezoid?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear method to determine the midsegment (median) of a trapezoid, including the definition, the formula (average of the lengths of the two bases), and step‑by‑step calculations or examples. Relevant documents should explain what the midsegment is, show how to locate the midpoints of the non‑parallel sides, provide the formula midsegment = ( base1 + base2 ) ÷ 2, and illustrate its use with concrete numeric problems.

title: AML Surveillance Analyst
description: What are the duties, required skills, and typical work environment of an AML surveillance analyst?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of the AML (anti‑money‑laundering) surveillance analyst role, including primary responsibilities such as monitoring transactions for suspicious activity, analyzing alerts, preparing reports, and ensuring compliance with regulations, as well as typical qualifications, certifications, and industry contexts. Relevant documents should describe job functions, required experience, and the regulatory backdrop of AML compliance.

title: Thai Daily Life
description: What are the typical day‑to‑day activities, routines, and cultural practices of ordinary people living in Thailand?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive picture of everyday life for average Thai residents, including work habits, family structures, meals, transportation, religious observances, leisure activities, and regional variations. Relevant material should describe how Thai people spend their time on weekdays and weekends, common occupations, social customs, and cultural norms, rather than isolated facts such as currency, flag symbolism, or monastic schedules.

title: SIGMET Definition
description: What is a SIGMET and what conditions trigger its issuance?
narrative: The user seeks a concise definition of a SIGMET, including its purpose in aviation weather reporting and the specific criteria (e.g., severe turbulence, severe icing, volcanic ash, dust, or embedded thunderstorms) that cause a SIGMET to be issued. Relevant information should describe both convective and non‑convective SIGMETs and the thresholds used by authorities such as the National Weather Service or regional FIR offices.

title: Concrete Flooring Cost
description: What are the typical per‑square‑foot costs for interior concrete flooring, including basic installations and polished or decorative finishes?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive pricing information for interior concrete floors. Relevant material should detail baseline costs (e.g., $2‑$6 per sq ft), added expenses for waterproofing, overlays, and sealing, and price variations for polished concrete based on grinding levels and decorative treatments, as well as any regional or contractor‑specific differences.

title: Swiss Popular Food
description: What is the most popular food or dish in Switzerland?
narrative: The user wants to know which food item is considered the most popular or most widely consumed in Switzerland. Relevant documents should mention a specific food or dish that is recognized nationally as the top favorite, supported by consumption statistics, surveys, cultural prominence, or frequent inclusion on traditional menus. Acceptable answers may cite items such as cheese fondue, chocolate, Rösti, or other dishes that are repeatedly identified as the leading favorite across Swiss regions.

title: Warm‑blooded Sharks
description: Which shark species are warm‑blooded and what physiological mechanisms allow them to keep their body temperature above ambient water?
narrative: The user seeks a concise scientific explanation of endothermy in sharks. Relevant documents must identify shark species that are warm‑blooded (e.g., great white, salmon shark, mako, thresher, other lamnids) and describe how they regulate temperature—such as counter‑current heat exchangers, insulated muscle tissue, and retia mirabilia. Sources that discuss the concept of endothermy in fish, compare it to ectothermy, or detail the temperature advantage for hunting are also relevant. Information unrelated to temperature regulation (e.g., size, diet, sightings, habitat without mention of thermoregulation) should be considered non‑relevant.

title: Durable Medical Equipment
description: What items are included in durable medical equipment?
narrative: The user wants a clear, comprehensive description of the categories and specific products that make up durable medical equipment (e.g., wheelchairs, oxygen concentrators, CPAP machines, hospital beds, etc.), especially those used at home and covered by Medicare. Relevant content should enumerate or exemplify these items and explain the scope of DME.

title: Exon Definition
description: What is an exon and what role does it play in gene structure and expression?
narrative: The user is looking for a concise, accurate definition of an exon in biological terms, including its function as the coding portion of a gene that is retained in mature mRNA, how it differs from introns, and its relevance to transcription, splicing, and protein synthesis. Relevant documents should explicitly describe exons, mention that they code for amino‑acid sequences, discuss their removal of introns during RNA processing, and may provide illustrative examples or context about gene architecture.

title: Visceral Definition
description: What is the meaning of the word “visceral”?
narrative: The user seeks a clear definition of the term “visceral,” including both its literal anatomical sense (relating to internal organs) and its figurative sense (relating to instinctive, gut-level feelings as opposed to intellectual reasoning). Relevant documents are those that provide dictionary‑style definitions, explain the origin of the word, and illustrate its usage in both medical and literary contexts.

title: Tracheid Location
description: What plant tissue or structure do tracheids belong to?
narrative: The user wants to complete the statement “tracheids are part of _____” with the correct anatomical term. Relevant passages must explicitly identify tracheids as components of the xylem (e.g., describing them as one of the two types of tracheary elements in vascular plants, part of xylem tissue, or a non‑living element of the xylem).

title: RSA Key Definition
description: What is RSA and what does the term 'key' mean in the context of RSA?
narrative: The user wants a clear, concise definition of the RSA public‑key cryptosystem together with an explanation of what a 'key' refers to—specifically the distinction between the public encryption key and the private decryption key. Relevant documents should describe RSA's purpose, its inventors, the basic mathematical basis, and explicitly identify the two keys used in the scheme.

title: CIS Formation
description: Who was responsible for forming the Commonwealth of Independent States?
narrative: The user seeks a factual answer identifying the person(s) who created the Commonwealth of Independent States after the dissolution of the Soviet Union. Relevant documents should clearly state that Boris Yeltsin, as President of the Russian Republic, along with the leaders of Ukraine and Belarus, convened in early December 1991 to establish the CIS. Any source that names these individuals or describes the formation meeting as the act of “forming” the CIS satisfies the information need.

title: LVH Causes
description: What are the causes of left ventricular hypertrophy?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive list of medical conditions, risk factors, and physiological mechanisms that lead to left ventricular hypertrophy, such as chronic hypertension, aortic valve stenosis, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, and other relevant etiologies. Relevant documents should explicitly identify and describe these causes, discuss their impact on cardiac structure, and may mention associated treatments or clinical implications.

title: LPS Act Definition
description: What is the definition and scope of the Lanterman‑Petris‑Short (LPS) law?
narrative: The user seeks a clear legal definition of the LPS (Lanterman‑Petris‑Short) Act, including its purpose, key provisions such as the 72‑hour involuntary hold, who may authorize it, and its historical context. Relevant documents should explicitly define the LPS Act, describe its application to mental health emergencies, and outline related statutes (e.g., WIC 5170) that govern involuntary detention.

title: Three Percenters Info
description: What is the Three Percenters organization, its history, beliefs, and activities?
narrative: The user wants a concise yet comprehensive overview of the Three Percenters militia movement, covering its origin, ideological foundations, relationship to other extremist groups, notable events, and ways to join or identify members. Relevant documents should explain who the Three Percenters claim to model after, describe their public presence, affiliations (e.g., with Oath Keepers or Minuteman Militia), and provide concrete details that define the group.

title: Military Suicide Causes
description: What factors contribute to suicide among military personnel?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of the underlying causes of suicide in the armed forces. Relevant material should identify and explain risk factors such as combat‑related trauma, PTSD, depression, anxiety, substance abuse (alcohol, prescription drugs), difficulties transitioning to civilian life, social isolation, and systemic or cultural issues within the military. Sources that provide epidemiological data only are only partially relevant; documents must discuss causal mechanisms or contributing conditions rather than merely listing suicide rates.

title: Theraderm Uses
description: What are the intended uses and benefits of Theraderm skin‑care products?
narrative: The user seeks a concise overview of Theraderm’s purpose, including the skin concerns it targets (e.g., anti‑aging, moisturization, acne treatment, sun‑damage repair) and the types of products offered. Relevant documents should describe Theraderm’s product lines, their advertised functions, and any clinical or consumer context that clarifies how the brand is meant to be used.

title: Famvir Indications
description: What medical conditions is Famvir (famciclovir) prescribed for?
narrative: The user wants to know the therapeutic uses of the antiviral drug Famvir, including which infections (e.g., shingles, genital herpes, cold sores) it is indicated to treat and any associated dosing guidance. Relevant documents should mention the conditions for which Famvir is prescribed, dosage recommendations, and the context of its use (e.g., early treatment of shingles, outbreak suppression for herpes).

title: Anthro Environment
description: What is the definition of ‘environment’ according to anthropology?
narrative: The user is looking for a scholarly, anthropological definition of the term “environment,” preferably one that relates environmental factors to human societies, culture, and social relations. Relevant documents should explicitly define or discuss “environment” within an anthropological framework, citing how it is understood in cultural or physical anthropology, and should distinguish this definition from purely ecological or geographical meanings.

title: Axon Terminal Definition
description: What is the definition and function of an axon terminal (also called a synaptic knob) in a neuron?
narrative: The user wants a concise, accurate description of the axon terminal/synaptic knob, including its anatomical location, role in releasing neurotransmitters into the synaptic cleft, and its relationship to the presynaptic neuron and synapse. Relevant passages should explicitly define the term, mention alternative names, and outline its functional significance in neuronal communication.

title: CDG Location
description: Is Charles de Gaulle Airport (CDG) located inside the main city of Paris?
narrative: The user wants to know whether CDG Airport lies within the central or administrative boundaries of Paris, rather than being a peripheral or out‑of‑city facility. Relevant documents should describe the geographic position of CDG relative to Paris proper (e.g., north of the city, distance from the city center), its administrative area, and any distinctions between the airport and central Paris.

title: Monotonic Function Example
description: What are some concrete examples of monotonic functions?
narrative: The user seeks specific mathematical functions that are monotonic (either strictly increasing or strictly decreasing) over their entire domain, along with brief explanations or illustrations of why they satisfy monotonicity. Relevant material should list such functions (e.g., f(x)=x³, f(x)=eˣ, f(x)=−x, f(x)=ln x on (0,∞), etc.) and describe their monotonic behavior.

title: Spruce Physical Description
description: What are the physical characteristics of spruce trees?
narrative: The user wants a detailed morphological description of spruce, covering traits such as needle shape, attachment, bark texture, cone size, overall growth form, and features that differentiate spruce from other conifers. Relevant passages should explicitly describe these physical attributes.

title: Hydrogen Liquid Temp
description: At what temperature does hydrogen become a liquid?
narrative: The user wants to know the temperature below which hydrogen exists as a liquid under normal pressure. Relevant passages must provide the liquefaction or boiling point of hydrogen (e.g., ~20.27 K, –252.9 °C, –423 °F) or any equivalent temperature indicating the gas‑to‑liquid transition. Documents that mention these temperature values, or that state the conditions (low temperature, possibly high pressure) required for hydrogen to be liquid, satisfy the information need.

title: McDouble vs Double
description: What are the differences between a McDouble and a Double Cheeseburger at McDonald's?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed comparison of the McDouble and the Double Cheeseburger, including ingredients (number of cheese slices and meat patties), price differences, menu placement, and any regional variations; documents that explain these distinctions are relevant.
